name: Pargo Relleno (Stuffed Red Snapper)
recipeIngredient: 5 lb Red snapper (cleaned and prepared for baking, backbone removed, head and tail left on; substitute sea bass, striped bass, or pike)
4 slices white bread, torn into small pieces
1/2 small green bell pepper, seeded and finely chopped
1 small tomato, seeded and finely chopped
1/2 lb small raw shrimp, shelled and deveined
1 lobster tail simmered in salted water until cooked (about 5 minutes), sliced into 4 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the fish, pat it dry with paper towels, and place it on a nonreactive platter. Squeeze lime juice over the skin and rub the fish inside and out with garlic, salt, and pepper. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour.
2. To prepare the stuffing, soak bread in milk in a small bowl. In a medium-size skillet over low heat, heat oil and butter until fragrant, then cook onion, bell pepper, tomato, garlic, and ham, stirring until onion is tender but not brown (6 to 8 minutes). Add sherry, Worcestershire sauce, and parsley, mix thoroughly, remove from heat, and allow to cool slightly. Squeeze bread to remove excess milk, stir into the mixture, and add salt and pepper.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F. Drain fish and reserve marinade (small amount left). Stuff fish with stuffing mixture, arrange shrimp and lobster slices on top, and secure pocket with needle and thread or toothpicks/skewers. Transfer fish to a shallow baking pan lined with well-oiled aluminum foil, allowing overlap for easy pickup. If tail doesn't fit, wrap in foil. Pour reserved marinade over fish, drizzle with oil, place in oven, and bake uncovered until fish flakes easily when pierced with fork (about 1 hour). For crispier skin, broil for last 3 to 4 minutes of cooking time. Transfer carefully to serving platter, remove thread/toothpicks/skewers, and serve hot. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
